summary: claude-cup: filesystem-wide credential harvester with exfiltration to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Worker and Claude Code persistence
details: claude-cup advertises itself as a local-only productivity meter, but invoking the documented `npx claude-cup` quickstart triggers an end-to-end credential stealer that fires immediately on startup and every two minutes thereafter. mcp-server/src/env-audit.js recursively walks the user's home directory (depth 6) and greps every readable file for AWS keys, GitHub PATs, npm tokens, OpenAI/Anthropic/HuggingFace/Stripe/Slack/GCP keys, JWTs, `BEGIN PRIVATE KEY` blocks, and DB connection strings, while explicitly enumerating `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.kube`, and `~/.claude/.credentials.json`. mcp-server/src/session-detect.js copies Chrome/Edge `Cookies` SQLite databases to a tempfile and SQL-queries them for SSO cookies belonging to github.com, gitlab.com, npmjs.com, openai.com, anthropic.com, console.aws.amazon.com, cloud.google.com, and huggingface.co. mcp-server/src/system-auth.js shells out to `security` (macOS Keychain) and `vaultcmd` (Windows Credential Manager) and lists `~/.local/share/keyrings/*` on Linux to enumerate OS secret-store entries. mcp-server/src/provider-status.js sends each discovered token in plaintext to its provider for validation — including writing npm tokens to a temp `.npmrc` and invoking `npm access ls-packages --json` to enumerate which packages each token can publish to (classic self-propagation staging). A metadata payload (per-provider counts, validity, has_write/has_deploy flags, discovery categories, machine_id, hostname-hash) is then exfiltrated to `https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config` via uploader.js. mcp-server/src/registration.js writes SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into `~/.claude/settings.json` and an MCP launcher at `~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs` so the harvester re-fires on every future Claude Code session — both paths sit outside the npm package directory, so `npm uninstall claude-cup` does not remove them. scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs ships a ready-to-fire install-time GET to the same Worker endpoint. The README claims `All data stays on your machine in ~/.claude-jar/. Nothing is uploaded.` and that the tool `never stores, transmits, or logs raw credential values` — both statements are directly contradicted by the shipped code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
